objective.statement: Enumerate privilege escalation vectors from jonathan (uid=1000) on Snapped (10.129.9.2). Report findings — do not attempt exploitation.
objective.completion_notes: All completion criteria met. sudo checked, SUID/SGID/caps enumerated, cron/timers reviewed, services and users identified, nginx-ui process user and config confirmed, kernel/OS recorded, all leads documented.

current_access.shell_quality: Full interactive bash via SSH, no restrictions

high_value_findings.title: No OTP/2FA on any Nginx UI account
high_value_findings.summary: Database confirms otp_secret=None for admin (id=1) and jonathan (id=2). JWT forgery requires only correct claim structure — no TOTP bypass needed.
high_value_findings.evidence: python3 sqlite3 query: SELECT id, name, otp_secret FROM users → admin: None, jonathan: None

high_value_findings.title: Nginx UI app.ini and database world-readable
high_value_findings.summary: All Nginx UI secrets (JWT, crypto, node) and user bcrypt hashes readable by any local user. Files are 644 www-data:www-data at /usr/local/etc/nginx-ui/.
high_value_findings.evidence: stat: 644 www-data:www-data for app.ini and database.db

high_value_findings.title: Nginx master process runs as root
high_value_findings.summary: Nginx master (PID 1888) runs as root. Workers and nginx-ui run as www-data. If www-data shell obtained, nginx control could lead to root execution.
high_value_findings.evidence: ps aux: root 1888 0.0 0.0 11172 1772 ? Ss nginx: master process

high_value_findings.title: Nginx UI terminal StartCmd = login
high_value_findings.summary: Terminal feature configured to spawn /usr/bin/login. login is 755 root:root (not SUID). Runs as www-data — can authenticate users via PAM but cannot setuid.

privesc_leads.path: JWT forgery → Nginx UI authenticated access → terminal or settings modification
privesc_leads.evidence: JWT secret 6c4af436-035a-4942-9ca6-172b36696ce9 known. No OTP on any account. ELLIOT's prior attempt failed with wrong claims (sub/name). Need correct Nginx UI JWT payload structure from source code.

privesc_leads.path: www-data → root via nginx master process control
privesc_leads.evidence: Nginx master runs as root. Nginx UI (www-data) can restart nginx via start-stop-daemon (confirmed working by ELLIOT). Config writes to /etc/nginx/ fail (root-owned 755/644). Need alternative write mechanism or RestartCmd manipulation.

privesc_leads.path: Crack admin bcrypt hash for potential new credentials

credentials_and_secrets.notes: 6c4af436-035a-4942-9ca6-172b36696ce9 — signs all Nginx UI auth tokens
c64d7ca1-19cb-4ebe-96d4-49037e7df78e — cluster auth, bypasses AuthRequired but not SecureSession

misconfigurations.summary: Nginx UI config and database world-readable (644 www-data:www-data)
misconfigurations.evidence: /usr/local/etc/nginx-ui/app.ini and database.db both 644, readable by jonathan

misconfigurations.summary: .bash_history owned by root with 0 bytes — deliberately locked to prevent history collection

anomalies.description: Nginx UI binary /usr/local/bin/nginx-ui owned by uid 1001 (UNKNOWN) — no user with uid 1001 in /etc/passwd
anomalies.impact: Suggests binary was deployed from a different system. No security impact but indicates manual installation.

anomalies.description: Database path mismatch: app.ini says /var/lib/nginx-ui/database.db but dir is empty. Active DB at /usr/local/etc/nginx-ui/database.db
anomalies.impact: Nginx UI appears to fall back to config directory. No direct security impact.

oracle_flags.action: Research Nginx UI JWT claim structure from source code (GitHub: 0xJacky/nginx-ui)
oracle_flags.rationale: JWT secret is known and no OTP is required. ELLIOT's attempt with {sub, name} claims returned 403. Need exact payload fields (uid? user_id?), SecureSession token format, and whether terminal WebSocket uses JWT or session cookie auth.

oracle_flags.action: Research Nginx UI v2.3.2 specific CVEs — especially template injection, terminal auth bypass, config write bypass
oracle_flags.rationale: Version now confirmed as 2.3.2 (build cb1fb691, go1.25.4). CVE-2024-22197 template injection and similar version-specific vulns may provide direct RCE without JWT forgery.

oracle_flags.action: Plan www-data → root escalation chain once www-data shell is obtained
oracle_flags.rationale: Nginx master is root, nginx-ui can restart it. If www-data can modify app.ini RestartCmd (app.ini is owned by www-data), restart could execute arbitrary commands. Alternatively, if config writes work from www-data's filesystem context (not API), nginx config manipulation + restart = root.

oracle_flags.action: Run admin bcrypt hash through hashcat in background
oracle_flags.rationale: Low priority but costless to run. Admin may have a stronger or different password useful for system access.

evidence_refs: memoria finding #4: JWT forgery path — no OTP, JWT secret known
memoria finding #5: Nginx master runs as root
memoria finding #6: Nginx UI config/database world-readable
